title: Implement Trust Layer human decision protocol

sources.reason: Defines Task 3 decision request persistence, stable IDs, resume conditions, and fresh Preflight requirements.
Defines required Decision Request identity, options, recommendation, question, and resume condition fields.
Defines the structured Decision Evidence identity and recorded-by fields.
Existing Preflight flow generates requests and validates hash-bound evidence.
Defines Human Decision State, Preflight Pause Rule, Evidence over Self-Declaration, and Governance Loop.

riskAssessment.reason: The protocol persists structured decision records and validates identity/hash freshness without executing external operations.

executionDecision.reason: Task 3 protocol boundaries and deterministic verification are explicit.

preReviewWarnings: Verify that chat text alone cannot satisfy confirmation, Decision Evidence is identity/hash bound, and resume requires a fresh ready Preflight.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Human Decision Requests are persisted under .ai/decisions/ with stable IDs and all schema-required fields.
Decision Evidence is rejected when decision ID, Work Item identity, Contract hash, or Preflight hash is stale or mismatched.
A valid decision does not resume execution until a fresh Preflight reports ready.
Positive, mismatch, persistence, chat-only, and rerun-state tests pass.
guidelines: Decision records are machine-readable and schema validated.
Decision protocol code must not infer approval from chat text or stale evidence.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: Preflight can generate a hash-bound Decision Request, but the current flow does not persist requests under a dedicated decision record, provide stable identity across reruns, or enforce a fresh ready Preflight after a decision.
constraints: Persist only structured, schema-valid decision records under .ai/decisions/.
Treat chat text as non-evidence and fail closed on stale or mismatched identity/hash fields.
Keep the protocol language-neutral and independent of any specific LLM.
intent.rationale: A durable, identity-bound decision protocol prevents accidental resume from stale or conversational confirmation and makes human intervention auditable.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized the sequential Trust Layer execution plan, including decision-protocol governance paths.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: A valid structured decision is persisted and a fresh ready Preflight can resume
Chat-only, stale, mismatched, or invalid decision evidence remains blocked
